{"query":"Venezuela May 2025 regional elections non-PSUV winning state governor name","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1094,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2025/5/26/venezuela-election-results-who-lost-won-and-what-next","title":"Venezuela election results: Who lost, won and what next? | Elections News | Al Jazeera","content":"Venezuela’s ruling coalition, led by President Nicolas Maduro, has won the parliamentary and regional elections by a landslide, maintaining a significant majority in the powerful National Assembly, according to the country’s electoral authority.
The Venezuelan government revised the electoral boundaries to elect a governor and eight representatives for the Essequibo, an oil-rich region that Caracas disputes with Guyana in a colonial-era dispute.
The vote took place in a micro-district of 21,403 voters in Venezuela’s Bolivar state, on the Guyanese border.
Guyana has administered the region for decades, but Caracas has threatened to partially annex it – a threat that Maduro repeated on Sunday.
Turnout in the elections was 8.9 million, or roughly 42 percent of 21 million voters eligible to cast their ballots, according to the CNE."},{"url":"https://venezuelanalysis.com/analysis/venezuelas-2025-legislative-and-regional-elections-a-quick-guide/","title":"Venezuela’s 2025 Legislative and Regional Elections: A Quick Guide - Venezuelanalysis","content":"Venezuelans return to the polls on Sunday, May 25, to elect a new National Assembly, governors for 24 states, and regional legislative councils.
This is the 32nd electoral event under the Bolivarian Revolution.
Voters will elect 285 deputies for five-year terms.
The Great Patriotic Pole (GPP) groups the ruling United Socialist Party (PSUV) and 12 allied organizations.
According to electoral spokespeople, some 21 thousand citizens are registered as Essequibo residents and eligible to vote in polling stations set up in Bolívar state.
Caracas rejected the International Court of Justice’s demand to suspend elections for Guayana Esequiba.
The CNE has also offered scarce updates, and no international observation missions are expected for the elections, although there will be hundreds of international observers.
Deputy Nicolás Maduro Guerra, the president’s son, leads the list in Caracas."},{"url":"https://www.as-coa.org/articles/jose-enrique-arrioja-venezuelas-regional-elections-latin-america-advisor","title":"José Enrique Arrioja on Venezuela's Regional Elections for the Latin America Advisor | AS/COA","content":"After the elections, Maduro’s government appointed a governor of the Essequibo region, which Guyana administers but Venezuela claims; Guyanese and U.S. officials condemned the act as a violation of international law."},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025_Venezuelan_parliamentary_election","title":"2025 Venezuelan parliamentary election - Wikipedia","content":"Parliamentary elections were held in Venezuela on 25 May 2025 to elect the 285 deputies of the National Assembly.
Official results showed that the ruling United Socialist Party of Venezuela (PSUV) of President Nicolás Maduro retained its majority in the National Assembly and won 23 of 24 state gubernatorial elections."},{"url":"https://www.france24.com/en/americas/20250526-venezuela-s-ruling-party-wins-landslide-in-elections-boycotted-by-opposition","title":"Venezuela's ruling party wins by landslide in elections boycotted by opposition - France 24","content":"Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro's socialist party on Sunday won heavily in parliamentary and regional elections that were boycotted by the opposition in protest over his disputed re-election last year.
This landslide win will keep the ruling party in control of the attorney general's office and the top court, whose members are elected by lawmakers.
The vote was for 285 members of the National Assembly and 24 governors – including for the first time in Essequibo, an oil-rich region controlled by neighbouring Guyana but claimed by Caracas.
Washington has also revoked deportation protection from 350,000 Venezuelan migrants in the United States and expelled hundreds of others to a maximum security prison in El Salvador."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"Venezuela May 2025 regional elections non-PSUV winning state governor name","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://venezuelanalysis.com/news/venezuela-pro-government-alliance-wins-big-in-legislative-and-regional-elections","title":"Venezuela: Pro-Government Alliance Wins Big in Legislative and Regional Elections - Venezuelanalysis","content":"# Venezuela: Pro-Government Alliance Wins Big in Legislative and Regional Elections. Caracas, May 26, 2025 (venezuelanalysis.com) – Venezuela’s ruling United Socialist Party (PSUV) and political allies emerged as overwhelming victors in Sunday’s regional and parliamentary elections. According to the country’s National Electoral Council (CNE), the Great Patriotic Pole received around 4.55 million votes, 82.7 percent of the total cast, in the election for the unicameral National Assembly. It was followed by the Democratic Alliance and UNT-Única, two opposition coalitions, with 6.25 and 5.18 percent, respectively. Far-right opposition factions led by María Corina Machado urged a boycott of the legislative and regional elections. The May 25 elections saw Venezuelan voters select a new 285-member National Assembly alongside 24 state governors and 260 regional legislators. Venezuela’s electoral authority delivered the first bulletin on Sunday night with a reported 93 percent of polling stations tallied. The turnout figure is in line with the previous legislative elections in 2020 that were likewise the subject of a partial opposition boycott.","score":0.709684,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.nytimes.com/2025/05/26/world/americas/venezuela-maduro-regional-election.html","title":"Venezuela's Government Claims Victory in Polls Boycotted by ...","content":"The announcement comes less than a year after a presidential election in which Mr. Maduro also claimed victory, despite a vote count that showed","score":0.62687886,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.france24.com/en/americas/20250526-venezuela-s-ruling-party-wins-landslide-in-elections-boycotted-by-opposition","title":"Venezuela's ruling party wins by landslide in elections boycotted by opposition - France 24","content":"# Venezuela's ruling party wins by landslide in elections boycotted by opposition. Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro's socialist party on Sunday won heavily in parliamentary and regional elections that were boycotted by the opposition in protest over his disputed re-election last year. More than 70 people were arrested in the lead-up to the election, including a leading opposition figure.
